Teacher.title: Teacher
Teacher.description: Represents a teacher's profile and associated data within the school system. Teacher login is managed by an administrator using an email and password.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Teacher entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the teacher.

lastName.description: The last name of the teacher.

email.description: The email address of the teacher, used for login and communication. (Managed by Admin, password handled by external auth system)

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the teacher.

address.description: The residential address of the teacher.

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the teacher.

hireDate.description: The date when the teacher was hired by the school.

subjectIds.description: References to Subjects taught by this teacher. (Relationship: Teacher N:N Subject)

classIds.description: References to Classes where this teacher provides instruction. (Relationship: Teacher N:N Class)

Student.title: Student
Student.description: Represents a student's profile and academic information. Student's NIS and an associated password (managed by admin) are used by parents for login.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Student entity.

nis.description: Nomor Induk Siswa (Student ID Number), a unique identifier for the student. This NIS, along with an admin-managed password, is used by parents for authentication.

firstName.description: The first name of the student.

lastName.description: The last name of the student.

dateOfBirth.description: The date of birth of the student.

gender.description: The gender of the student (e.g., Male, Female, Other).

address.description: The residential address of the student.

noWa.description: The WhatsApp contact number of the student or their guardian.

enrollmentDate.description: The date when the student was enrolled in the school.

classId.description: Reference to the Class the student belongs to. (Relationship: Class 1:N Student)

parentIds.description: References to Parents/Guardians associated with this student. (Relationship: Student N:N Parent)

Parent.title: Parent
Parent.description: Represents a parent or guardian's profile associated with students. Parents log in using their child's NIS and an admin-provided password.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Parent entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the parent or guardian.

lastName.description: The last name of the parent or guardian.

email.description: The email address of the parent or guardian for communication purposes.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number of the parent or guardian.

address.description: The residential address of the parent or guardian.

relationshipToStudent.description: The relationship of the parent/guardian to the student (e.g., 'Mother', 'Father', 'Guardian').

studentIds.description: References to Students associated with this parent/guardian. (Relationship: Parent N:N Student)

Class.title: Class
Class.description: Represents a specific class or grade level within the school.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Class entity.

academicYear.description: The academic year this class belongs to (e.g., '2023-2024').

homeroomTeacherId.description: Reference to the homeroom teacher for this class. (Relationship: Teacher 1:N Class)

Subject.title: Subject
Subject.description: Represents an academic subject taught in the school.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Subject entity.

description.description: A brief description of the subject content.

Curriculum.title: Curriculum
Curriculum.description: Represents a single curriculum entry, associating a subject with a class level and a specific book.

id.description: Unique identifier for the curriculum entry.

subjectCode.description: The unique code for the subject.

subjectName.description: The name of the subject (Mata Pelajaran).

classLevel.description: The class level this curriculum applies to (e.g., 0, 1, 2, ... 6).

bookName.description: The name of the textbook or 'Kitab' used for this subject at this class level.

Alumni.title: Alumni
Alumni.description: Represents a former student who has graduated.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Alumni entity, same as original student ID.

nis.description: Nomor Induk Siswa (Student ID Number) of the alumni.

name.description: The full name of the alumni.

tahunLulus.description: The academic year the student graduated (e.g., '2023-2024').

address.description: The last known residential address of the alumni.

noWa.description: The last known WhatsApp number of the alumni.

reportUrl.description: URL to the student's final digital report card.

definition.description: Dedicated collection for administrative users. The existence of a document at `/roles_admin/{adminUid}` grants admin privileges to `adminUid`. This adheres to the DBAC principle 'Existence over Content'. Note: The schema reference points to 'Teacher' as a placeholder for a user-like entity, as a specific 'Admin' entity was not provided in the backend entities. Only the document ID (`adminUid`) is relevant for authorization.

params.name: adminUid
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the administrator.

definition.description: Stores teacher profiles. The document ID (`teacherId`) is the Firebase Authentication UID of the teacher, enabling direct path-based ownership authorization. Admin has full CRUD. Teachers can read and update their own profile. Includes `classIds` which are used for teachers to list students in their classes (QAPs).

params.name: teacherId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the teacher.

definition.description: Stores parent profiles. The document ID (`parentId`) is the Firebase Authentication UID of the parent. Admin has full CRUD. Parents can read and update their own profile. Includes `studentIds` for linking to children.

params.name: parentId
params.description: The Firebase Authentication UID of the parent.

definition.description: Stores student records. Admin has full CRUD. Parents can read student data if their UID is listed in the denormalized `parentUids` array. Teachers can read student data if the student's `classId` is one of the classes they teach. This includes denormalized `parentUids` (array of Firebase UIDs of parents) for Authorization Independence, simplifying parent-specific `list` queries and rules (QAPs).

params.name: studentId
params.description: The unique ID of the student.

definition.description: Stores class details. Admin has full CRUD. Teachers can read all classes and update classes where they are the `homeroomTeacherId`. Parents can read any class data. Provides `homeroomTeacherId` for teacher-specific authorization and QAPs.

params.name: classId
params.description: The unique ID of the class.

definition.description: Stores academic subject details. Admin has full CRUD. All authenticated users (Teachers, Parents) can read subject data for reference.

params.name: subjectId
params.description: The unique ID of the subject.

definition.description: Stores the school's curriculum. Admin has full CRUD. Teachers and parents can read.

params.name: curriculumId
params.description: The unique ID of the curriculum entry.

definition.description: Stores records of graduated students (alumni). Admin has full CRUD. Other authenticated users can read.

params.name: alumniId
params.description: The unique ID of the alumni, which is the original student ID (NIS).

firestore.reasoning:
The recommended Firestore structure for EduConnect prioritizes secure, scalable, and debuggable authorization by adhering strictly to the core design principles and strategy mandates. 

**Authorization Independence (CRITICAL):**
1.  **Denormalized Parent UIDs in Student Documents:** Each `Student` document in `/students/{studentId}` will include a denormalized array, `parentUids`, storing the Firebase Authentication UIDs of all associated parents. This eliminates the need for `get()` calls in security rules to check parent-student relationships. A parent can directly read their children's records by checking `request.auth.uid in resource.data.parentUids` on the student document, making rules atomic and easy to debug.
2.  **Path-Based Ownership for User Profiles:** `Teacher` and `Parent` profiles are stored in `/teachers/{teacherId}` and `/parents/{parentId}` respectively, where `{teacherId}` and `{parentId}` directly correspond to the Firebase Authentication UID of the authenticated user. This allows direct path validation (`request.auth.uid == teacherId` or `request.auth.uid == parentId`) for accessing one's own profile, ensuring strong authorization independence.
3.  **Homeroom Teacher ID in Class Documents:** `Class` documents include `homeroomTeacherId`. This allows a homeroom teacher to update their specific class by checking `resource.data.homeroomTeacherId == request.auth.uid`, avoiding `get()` calls for class-specific write permissions.
4.  **Acceptable `get()` usage:** While strictly avoiding `get()` calls for authorization is paramount, `get()` calls on the *user's own profile* (e.g., `get(/teachers/$(request.auth.uid))`) to retrieve their capabilities (like `classIds` they teach) are considered acceptable for Authorization Independence. These operations do not introduce the same transactional integrity or debugging complexities as `get()` calls to unrelated or hierarchically dependent documents for authorization decisions.

**QAPs (Rules are not Filters):**
1.  **For Parents:** Parents can efficiently query for *their* students using a simple `list` operation: `/students.where("parentUids", "array-contains", request.auth.uid)`. This is directly supported by the denormalized `parentUids` field.
2.  **For Teachers:**
    *   Teachers can query for *their own* profiles using `id == request.auth.uid`.
    *   Teachers can query for *their homeroom classes*: `/classes.where("homeroomTeacherId", "==", request.auth.uid)`.
    *   To list students in their assigned classes, the client application can first retrieve the `classIds` from the teacher's own profile (`/teachers/{teacherId}`) and then perform a query like `/students.where("classId", "in", teacher.classIds)`. Security rules will then verify that `request.auth.uid` corresponds to a teacher and that `resource.data.classId` is one of the classes they are authorized to access (potentially using a `get()` on their own teacher profile).
3.  **For Admin:** Admin roles are determined by the mere existence of a document in `/roles_admin/{adminUid}`, enabling straightforward checks for full CRUD access across all collections.
4.  **Global Read Access for Reference Data:** `Classes` and `Subjects` collections are structured to be broadly readable by all authenticated users (Teachers, Parents) for reference purposes, simplifying `list` queries without complex, filtering security rules.

**Structural Segregation (Homogeneous Security Posture):**
Each entity type (`Teacher`, `Parent`, `Student`, `Class`, `Subject`) resides in its own top-level collection (`/teachers`, `/parents`, `/students`, `/classes`, `/subjects`). This ensures that all documents within a collection share a consistent security posture, simplifying security rules and enhancing clarity.

**Access Modeling (DBAC & Consistency):**
1.  **DBAC (Database-Backed Access Control):** Admin roles are managed via a dedicated collection `/roles_admin/{adminUid}`. The existence of a document signifies administrative privileges, adhering to the 'Existence over Content' principle.
2.  **Path-Based Ownership:** Used for `Teacher` and `Parent` profiles where the document ID is the user's Firebase UID.
3.  **Membership Map (Denormalized):** `parentUids` in `Student` documents acts as a denormalized membership map for parental access.

**Data Clarity and Predictability:**
Strict naming conventions (`{teacherId}`, `{studentId}`) and explicit field usage (e.g., `parentUids` for denormalized parent UIDs) ensure clarity and maintainability.
